series_context.status: SAS defeated OKC for third time in two weeks
prior_meetings: SAS 111, OKC 109 on 2025-12-13 NBA Cup Semifinal
series_context.okc_context: OKC entered as defending NBA champion and suffered first home loss of season. Thunder are 2-4 since 24-1 start, with three losses to SAS.

OKC.close_result: OKC scored 102; under all listed TT bands cashed by 20+ points

closing_result_eval.moneyline: SAS upset ML won at roughly +260 to +321 range
closing_result_eval.spread: SAS +9.5/+10.5 covered outright by 15; OKC -9/-10.5 failed hard

line_read_for_model.market_assumption: Despite two recent SAS wins, market still held OKC as a large home favorite near -9 to -10.5 and priced OKC team total above 122.
line_read_for_model.model_postmortem_correction: This created a third consecutive market misprice: SAS spread/ML resilience and OKC team-total suppression repeated.
line_read_for_model.edge_classification: SAS spread, SAS ML sprinkle, OKC team total under, and full-game under were all supported by matchup-specific suppression signals. SAS team total over also cleared, but with less margin than Dec 23.

q1.summary: OKC opened hot, made first seven field goals, and led by 8. Fox immediately countered with 13 first-quarter points, Castle pressure, and Wembanyama/Keldon bench surge flipped the quarter. SAS shot 71.4% in Q1.

q2.summary: Spurs stabilized after OKC bench pressure. Fox reached 21 first-half points and SAS led 69-60 at halftime. OKC shot only 38.1% in Q2 and failed to generate enough clean secondary offense.

q3.summary: Decisive suppression quarter. OKC shot 7/26 and 1/12 from three. Wembanyama three, Barnes three, and Harper dunk extended the gap to 17. SAS led 95-79 after three.

q4.summary: OKC briefly cut it to 9, but Wembanyama's 26-foot three restored separation. Spurs managed clock and maintained double-digit control. Garbage-time scoring landed final at 117-102.

De'Aaron Fox.note: Primary first-half stabilizer. His 21 first-half points erased OKC's opening surge and set the Spurs game script.

Victor Wembanyama.note: Returned full matchup gravity with 11 rebounds and spacing. His 4Q three restored separation after OKC cut the lead.

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander.note: Held to 36.8% FG and 6 second-half points; SAS length forced a low-efficiency night.

sas_road_resilience.note: SAS won outright at OKC despite being priced as +260 to +321 underdog.

sas_shot_quality_conversion.note: SAS generated and converted high-value looks; OKC shot quality was similar but conversion collapsed.

okc_conversion_collapse.note: OKC's expected shot diet did not convert under SAS length and contest pressure.

okc_offensive_rebounding_salvage.note: OKC generated second-chance volume but still finished 102 because primary shotmaking collapsed.

rolling_window_updates.series_signal: SAS has now produced three distinct win scripts vs OKC: Cup close-game survival, Dec 23 Q4 avalanche, and Dec 25 road halfcourt suppression blowout.
rolling_window_updates.model_implication: OKC cannot be priced as a standard double-digit favorite against this SAS ecosystem without a major adjustment. The recurring profile is SAS length suppressing OKC efficiency, Fox/Castle creating enough offense, and Wembanyama geometry breaking OKC's normal defensive pressure shell.

spread.postmortem_adjustment: Major downgrade to OKC cover probability; SAS plus-points band must be upgraded materially
spread.status: SAS spread resilience confirmed for third straight matchup

moneyline.note: SAS ML upset at +260 to +321 confirms matchup-specific upset tail is not negligible.

total.note: Under profile is stronger when SAS suppresses OKC efficiency without creating extreme 43-point Q4 avalanche like Dec 23.

team_totals.sas_tt: upgrade if line <=112.5 with Fox/Wemby/Castle active
team_totals.okc_tt: downgrade aggressively if priced above 121.5/122.5 vs SAS length and halfcourt pressure

player_props.Fox_points: watchlist vs OKC early counterpunch role
player_props.OKC_3PM_volume: under efficiency risk despite high attempts
